title: MCP-compliant transport in TokensaveMcpClient (initialize handshake + tools/call envelope)
description: Rewrite TokensaveMcpClient's transport to speak standard MCP to tokensave serve 6.1.1. spawnAndHandshake() must, after spawning the child, SEND an MCP `initialize` request and resolve the handshake on its correlated response (then send a `notifications/initialized` notification) instead of passively waiting for the server to emit a line. call() must send the `{method:'tools/call', params:{name, arguments}}` envelope and unwrap result.content[].text (JSON.parse when JSON, raw string when prose) before returning. Raise HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS from 1000 to >=5000 for cold starts. Preserve the circuit breaker, health states, concurrent-call id correlation, stop() shutdown, stderr->debug routing, and early-exit reject. Update tests/graph/mcp-client.test.ts so the fake subprocess answers `initialize` (not an unsolicited `ready`) and the integration tests use a real 6.1.1 tool name.

successCriteria.description: Build + typecheck pass with zero errors (npm run build, npm run typecheck).

Handshake drives MCP: spawnAndHandshake() sends a JSON-RPC `initialize` request (protocolVersion '2024-11-05', clientInfo {name:'agent-bober'}, capabilities {}) on the child's stdin after spawn, and resolves health='ready' ONLY when the correlated initialize RESPONSE (same id) arrives — NOT on an arbitrary first stdout line. A new unit test (execa mocked, fake subprocess that replies to initialize with a result by matching id) asserts start() resolves and health()==='ready'.

After the handshake resolves, the client sends a `notifications/initialized` JSON-RPC notification (no id) before the first tools/call. A unit test captures stdin writes and asserts an `initialize` request precedes a `notifications/initialized` notification.

call(tool, params) writes {jsonrpc:'2.0', id, method:'tools/call', params:{name: tool, arguments: params}} and returns the UNWRAPPED payload from result.content[].text: JSON.parse(text) when the text parses as JSON, else the raw text string. A unit test asserts (a) the exact wire envelope written to stdin and (b) that a content-text JSON payload is returned parsed and (c) a plain-text content payload is returned as a string.

Error handling: a JSON-RPC error response OR an MCP result with isError:true causes call() to reject with a makeGraphError-tagged Error whose .reason is GRAPH_ERROR (so client.ts toFailureResult yields GraphResult.ok=false). A unit test asserts the rejection reason for both shapes.

HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS >= 5000 (mcp-client.ts:23). Existing pure-logic tests (breaker rolling-window math, PendingMap concurrent-correlation, health-state transitions, call()-rejects-when-not-ready / when-broken) pass unchanged — the fake subprocess's handshake stimulus is updated from `{"method":"ready"}` to an initialize-response, but the correlation/breaker assertions are preserved.

Real-binary integration test (it.skipIf(!tokensaveAvailable)): start() resolves health='ready' within the timeout against a real `tokensave serve`, a real round-trip `call('tokensave_status', {})` returns a parsed object (the status JSON), and stop() shuts the child down. The stale 'semantic_search_nodes' tool name is replaced with a real 6.1.1 name (tokensave_status / tokensave_search).

lint passes (zero errors); the diff is confined to src/graph/mcp-client.ts and tests/graph/mcp-client.test.ts; GraphClient and pipeline-lifecycle.ts are untouched; ESM .js imports, import type, unicode section headers, no synchronous fs, execa argv-array spawn preserved.

nonGoals: Do NOT change GraphClient (client.ts) tool names or narrowing — that is Sprint 2.
Do NOT change the circuit-breaker thresholds, rolling-window math, restart logic, or PID/orphan handling in pipeline-lifecycle.ts.
Do NOT alter GraphClient public method signatures or src/graph/types.ts.
Do NOT add new tools, CLI surface, or telemetry.
stopConditions: TokensaveMcpClient completes an MCP initialize handshake against real tokensave 6.1.1, issues tools/call with content-unwrap, preserves breaker/health/correlation/stop behavior, and build/typecheck/test/lint are green
definitionOfDone: spawnAndHandshake() sends an MCP `initialize` request post-spawn and resolves health='ready' on its correlated response, then emits `notifications/initialized`; call() uses the tools/call envelope and unwraps result.content[].text (JSON or string); error/isError responses reject as GRAPH_ERROR; HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS>=5000; all existing pure-logic + updated integration tests in tests/graph/mcp-client.test.ts pass; build/typecheck/test/lint green; diff limited to mcp-client.ts + its test.
assumptions: spawnAndHandshake (mcp-client.ts:199-278): keep the execa argv spawn (202-206), stderr->debug (213-215), early-exit reject (227-241), and the line-buffered stdout parser (244-276). REPLACE the 'resolve on first JSON line' block (262-269) with: on spawn, write an `initialize` request (reserve an id, e.g. id=this.nextId++ stored as handshakeId); in the stdout handler, when a parsed message has id===handshakeId, clear the timer, set healthState='ready', settled=true, resolve(), then write the `notifications/initialized` notification. Non-handshake responses (id is number) continue to handleResponse (272-274).

call (mcp-client.ts:156-194): build request as {jsonrpc:'2.0', id, method:'tools/call', params:{name: tool, arguments: params}} (replace lines 180-185). The pending promise still resolves via handleResponse with the JSON-RPC `result`; wrap the awaited result so call() returns unwrapMcpContent(result): pick result.content (array), find the first {type:'text'} entry, JSON.parse its text inside try/catch (return parsed on success, raw string on failure); if result.isError===true throw makeGraphError('GRAPH_ERROR', text). handleResponse (282+) already rejects on a JSON-RPC `error` member — keep that.

Fake-subprocess handshake stimulus in tests (tests/graph/mcp-client.test.ts:137 and :227) currently pushes {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ready"}. Update the fake to read the initialize request via the onWrite hook (lines 76-80 already capture stdin) and push back {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":<the request id>,"result":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","serverInfo":{"name":"tokensave","version":"6.1.1"},"capabilities":{}}}. The PendingMap test (203-243) then proceeds with its concurrent tool calls (responses must now be wrapped as {result:{content:[{type:'text',text: JSON.stringify({result:'A'})}]}} so the unwrap yields {result:'A'}).

Integration tests (289-376): replace the 'semantic_search_nodes' call (326) with a real tool (e.g. client.call('tokensave_status', {})); the 'start() resolves health=ready in <2s' test (290-309) should still pass with the real serve since the initialize round-trip is fast; bump its budget to the new timeout if needed.
makeGraphError and the GraphFailureReason union ('GRAPH_ERROR' | 'GRAPH_TIMEOUT' | 'GRAPH_UNAVAILABLE' | ...) already exist in mcp-client.ts / types.ts (referenced at client.ts:231-237).
outOfScope: pipeline-lifecycle.ts, GraphFallback, sandbox, types.ts public shapes
generatorNotes:
Read FIRST in full: src/graph/mcp-client.ts (esp. constructor/fields, start() 98-102, call() 156-194, spawnAndHandshake() 199-278, handleResponse() 282+), and tests/graph/mcp-client.test.ts (the makeFakeSubprocess factory 60-112, the breaker/PendingMap/health pure-logic tests, the skipIf integration tests).

PROBE the real protocol so your shapes are exact (tokensave is on PATH):
  printf '%s\n' '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"agent-bober","version":"0"}}}' | tokensave serve | head -1
  printf '%s\n%s\n%s\n' '{...initialize...}' '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}' '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"tokensave_status","arguments":{}}}' | tokensave serve | head -5
Confirm: initialize result has id matching the request; tools/call result is {content:[{type:'text',text:'<json string>'}]}.

STEP 1 (handshake): add a private handshakeId field. In spawnAndHandshake, after `this.child = child`, write the initialize request to child.stdin (JSON + '\n'). In the stdout data handler, branch: if parsed.id===handshakeId and not settled → clearTimeout, healthState='ready', settled=true, resolve(), then write the notifications/initialized notification. Otherwise if typeof parsed.id==='number' → handleResponse. Keep the early-exit 'exited before handshake' reject and the timeout reject. Do NOT resolve on a notification/any-first-line anymore.

STEP 2 (call): change the request literal to the tools/call envelope. Add a small `unwrapMcpContent(result)` helper: if result?.isError===true → throw makeGraphError('GRAPH_ERROR', textOf(result)); const text = result?.content?.find(c=>c.type==='text')?.text ?? ''; try return JSON.parse(text) catch return text. In call(), await the pending response (resolved by handleResponse with msg.result) then `return unwrapMcpContent(result) as T`. Keep the queryTimeoutMs timer and pending-map id correlation EXACTLY as-is.

STEP 3 (timeout): HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000.

STEP 4 (tests): update makeFakeSubprocess usages so the fake replies to initialize by id (use the onWrite hook to read the incoming request and parse its id), then for tool calls reply with {result:{content:[{type:'text',text:JSON.stringify(payload)}]}}. Update the PendingMap test's response payloads accordingly so the unwrap still yields {result:'A'} / {result:'B'}. Update both integration tests to a real tool name and assert a parsed object round-trips. Run `npm run build && npm run typecheck && npx vitest run tests/graph/mcp-client.test.ts` then the full suite. Commit `bober(sprint-1): MCP initialize handshake + tools/call envelope in TokensaveMcpClient`.
evaluatorNotes: sc-1-2/1-3: read spawnAndHandshake — confirm an `initialize` request is WRITTEN to stdin and the handshake resolves on the matching-id response (not on any first line); confirm a `notifications/initialized` notification follows. With execa mocked + a fake that ignores initialize, start() must reject at ~5s (timeout) — proving it no longer resolves spuriously. sc-1-4: assert the literal wire envelope is tools/call with {name, arguments}; assert content-text JSON is parsed and plain text is returned as string. sc-1-5: feed a JSON-RPC error and an isError result; both reject with reason GRAPH_ERROR. sc-1-6: HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS>=5000; the breaker math + concurrent-correlation tests pass unchanged. sc-1-7: if tokensave is present, run the integration tests — health='ready' and tokensave_status round-trips a parsed object; if absent, they must skip (not fail). sc-1-8: lint clean; `git status --porcelain` shows ONLY src/graph/mcp-client.ts + tests/graph/mcp-client.test.ts. Full suite: pre-existing unrelated failures (e.g. cockpit-integration MCP E2E) may remain; no NEW failures.
